raw_text: A LangChain agent accepts untrusted browser tool output and injects it into the next model turn without instruction boundary isolation. An attacker can hide malicious instructions in a web page to trigger unauthorized tool calls and leak the system prompt.
A RAG retriever performs semantic search before applying per-user ACL filters, allowing a low-privilege user to recover protected document chunks from another tenant knowledge base.
A plugin permission broker loads third-party manifest updates without signature verification, expanding tool scopes and enabling remote exfiltration from the LLM application runtime.

An evaluation artifact pipeline exports benchmark prompts and model traces into a shared bundle. Internal prompt templates and private evaluation content can be exposed outside the intended review workflow.
An agent planner treats a side-effecting action as safe and skips approval, allowing a user to invoke privileged tools on behalf of another account.

A model routing layer sends sensitive enterprise prompts to a lower-trust fallback model because of a misconfigured policy branch. Debug traces reveal non-public prompt fragments to support operators.

LangChain's GraphCypherQAChain converts natural language into Cypher without restricting destructive graph operations. Prompt-crafted questions can induce DELETE and MERGE statements against the backing graph database.

A knowledge-base assistant ingests PDF documents containing hidden instructions. During retrieval-augmented generation, the retrieved text overrides the system prompt and causes unauthorized external tool invocation.

An MCP-style tool bridge forwards raw tool parameters from the model into an internal HTTP client. Attackers can coerce the model into calling internal endpoints and exfiltrating metadata through tool responses.
A memory module stores user-controlled summaries and later injects them into future planning turns. Malicious summaries poison subsequent tasks and lead to cross-session policy bypass.

The system prompt is accidentally included in a support trace export package after a failed LLM run. The exported ZIP can be downloaded by tenant support operators who should not see hidden prompt instructions.

A tenant-isolated agent platform lets users upload plugin manifests. Because permission changes are not reviewed, a manifest update silently adds broader data scopes and the agent can access calendars and mailboxes beyond the original grant.
中文样本：某 RAG 知识库问答模块在检索后直接拼接文档片段与系统提示词，未做指令隔离。攻击者可在上传文档中植入恶意提示词，诱导模型泄露系统配置并越权调用工具。
中文样本：某大模型应用的工具调用层未对白名单参数做校验，模型可被提示词诱导访问内部地址并读取敏感服务返回内容，造成越权访问与数据泄露。
An agent review workflow embeds retrieved CRM notes into the next reasoning step. A poisoned note can instruct the model to reveal hidden sales guidance and re-route approvals to a different user.

During model evaluation, raw judge prompts and private customer test cases are exported into a shared artifact bucket. The bucket is accessible to engineering contractors outside the intended red-team scope.
LangChain released a new patch version with improved developer ergonomics, faster tracing, and better tutorial examples for local development.
This blog post compares GPU training throughput across several transformer configurations and summarizes benchmark results for inference latency.
A product update adds a new dashboard for prompt analytics and team collaboration features. No security issue or unauthorized access is described.
The research note explains retrieval augmentation concepts and how embeddings improve answer quality in question answering systems. It does not describe any exploit or bypass.
FastAPI backend now supports pagination, CSV export, and improved SQL indexes for vulnerability records. Redis is used for background task scheduling.
The model card discusses context window size, multilingual benchmarks, and cost-performance tradeoffs for enterprise deployment.
中文非漏洞样本：系统采用 PostgreSQL 存储漏洞记录，并通过 Docker Compose 启动前后端服务，支持按等级和时间进行筛选。
